prompt: Create a clean classroom-ready infographic comparing major exoplanet classes. Subject: comparison of exoplanet classes — hot Jupiter, super-Earth, mini-Neptune, water world, rocky Earth-like; arranged as a clear comparative panel system; five labeled modules. Scene: clean educational space backdrop, contemporary science communication, soft dark space field with restrained stars and clean grid logic. World logic: panel readability must dominate over spectacle, labels should sit near the corresponding planet modules, the backdrop should support contrast without clutter. Composition: educational infographic, wide horizontal modular layout with title at top and five comparison panels below, orthographic-inspired flat presentation, 35mm lens feel, minimal perspective distortion for clarity, negative space: clean top band and gutters between panels, aspect ratio 16:9. Lighting: key light: soft even panel lighting, fill: balanced diffuse illumination, rim: subtle edge glow on planet icons only, clean digital clarity without heavy bloom, material response: smooth stylized planet surfaces, flat label panels, gentle icon highlights, neutral-cool educational palette. Style stack: stylized-3d, sci-fi, infographic, text-in-image, friendly clean explainer rendering, curious and clear, palette: deep navy, teal, gold accents, warm coral highlights, soft white, museum science graphics traits: modular spacing, trustworthy labels, gentle depth without clutter. On-image text: headline: Exoplanet Classes at a Glance; label: Hot Jupiter; label: Super-Earth; label: Mini-Neptune; label: Water World; label: Rocky Earth-like; body: Compare size, atmosphere, and likely composition.. Text layout: headline in top band, one label per planet panel, body line under title, 6% outer margins with strong gutters between modules, en. Must include: five distinct planet modules, clear labels near each module, classroom-ready hierarchy. Avoid: dense star clutter, tiny unreadable labels, overly dramatic cinematic lighting, masterpiece, best quality. Accessibility: high contrast text, clear color separation between modules.

thinking_config.note: Use chat/session history for multi-turn edits so thought signatures are preserved by the SDK.

metadata.provenance: Gemini-generated or Gemini-edited output; SynthID watermark expected for generated images.
